ogTitle: Picton East vs Piesseville: Climate Safety

heading: Picton East vs Piesseville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Picton East, Western Australia and Piesseville, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Piesseville has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Picton East (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Picton East or Piesseville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Picton East has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Piesseville scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Picton East scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Piesseville scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Picton East or Piesseville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Piesseville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
